title: Making plans with a friend
canDo: I can invite a friend out, arrange a time, and politely change a plan.

chunks.text: What are you up to this weekend?
chunks.example: Hey, what are you up to this weekend?

chunks.text: Do you fancy grabbing a coffee?
chunks.example: Do you fancy grabbing a coffee after work?
I'm free on Saturday afternoon, if that works.
Let's meet around noon at the usual place.

chunks.text: Something came up, can we reschedule?
chunks.use: Politely move or cancel a plan.
chunks.example: Sorry, something came up, can we reschedule?

chunks.text: Can I take a rain check?
chunks.use: Politely decline now but suggest another time.
chunks.example: I can't tonight, can I take a rain check?

reading.passage: Maya texts her friend: what are you up to this weekend? I'm meeting a few people on Saturday. Do you fancy grabbing a coffee first? I'm free on Saturday afternoon. Liam replies: sounds good, let's meet around noon. An hour later he writes: something came up, can we reschedule?

script.t: What are you up to this weekend?
Not much. Do you fancy grabbing a coffee?

gist.q: What do they agree to do?

fluency432.prompt: Tell me about your typical weekend.

retell.prompt: Retell Maya and Liam's chat in your own words.

task.prompt: Invite a friend out, suggest a time, then change the plan politely.

writing.model: Hi Sam! What are you up to this weekend? Do you fancy grabbing a coffee? I'm free on Saturday afternoon, let's meet around noon.

sentenceCombining.answer: I'm free on Saturday, so let's meet around noon.
Something came up, so I can't meet today.

task.prompt: Write a short message inviting a friend out this weekend.

quiz.q: You want to ask a friend about their weekend plans.
options: What are you up to this weekend?
Can I take a rain check?

quiz.q: You can't make it and want to move the plan.
options: Something came up, can we reschedule?

title: Opinions
canDo: I can give an opinion and politely agree or disagree.

chunks.text: In my opinion, it's worth it.
chunks.example: In my opinion, it's worth it.
I see your point, but I'm not sure.

chunks.text: I'm not so sure about that.
chunks.example: I'm not so sure about that.
That makes sense, actually.
It depends on the situation.

grammar.pattern: I see your point, but... / I'm not so sure...

reading.passage: One friend says online study is better. The other replies: I see your point, but I'm not so sure. A classroom helps me focus. The first nods: that makes sense, it depends on the person.

questions.q: What does the second speaker prefer?

script.t: In my opinion, it's worth it.
I see your point, but I'm not so sure.

fluency432.prompt: Give your opinion on online study, then consider the other side.

retell.prompt: Retell the discussion about online study.

task.prompt: Discuss whether phones help or harm learning; agree and disagree politely.

writing.model: In my opinion, online study is flexible. I see your point about focus, but it depends on the person.

sentenceCombining.answer: I see your point, but I'm not so sure.
It's cheaper, but it depends on the quality.

task.prompt: Write a short opinion (3 sentences): your view plus one counter-point.

options: In my opinion, it's worth it.
I'm not so sure about that.

title: At work
canDo: I can arrange a meeting and explain I'm busy, using the first conditional.

chunks.example: I'll get back to you by Friday.

chunks.text: Can we set up a meeting?
chunks.example: Can we set up a meeting this week?

chunks.use: Say you're not up to date.
chunks.example: I'm a bit behind on the report.
Let's touch base on Monday.

chunks.text: If I have time, I'll help.
chunks.example: If I have time, I'll help you finish.

examples: If I finish early, I'll call.
If it works, we'll keep it.

reading.passage: One colleague asks: can we set up a meeting? The other replies: sure, but I'm a bit behind this week. If I have time, I'll prepare the report. Let's touch base on Monday, and I'll get back to you with a time.

questions.q: Why is it hard to meet now?

script.t: Can we set up a meeting?
Sure. If I have time, I'll send a slot today.

gist.q: Will B send a meeting time?

fluency432.prompt: Explain you're busy this week and propose a meeting time.

retell.prompt: Retell the conversation about setting up a meeting.

task.prompt: Arrange a work meeting; say you're busy and propose Monday.

writing.model: Can we set up a meeting? I'm a bit behind, so let's touch base on Monday. I'll get back to you with a time.

sentenceCombining.answer: I'm a bit behind, so let's meet on Monday.
If I have time, I'll help.

task.prompt: Write a short work email proposing a meeting (3 sentences).

options: Can we set up a meeting?

title: Travel problems
canDo: I can report a problem and ask for it to be fixed.

chunks.example: My flight was delayed by two hours.

chunks.text: I'd like to make a complaint.
chunks.example: I'd like to make a complaint, please.
There's been a mix-up with my booking.
Could you sort it out for me?
The card machine isn't working.

reading.passage: A traveller says: my flight was delayed and there's been a mix-up with my booking. I'd like to make a complaint, and the app isn't working either. The staff member replies: I'm sorry, I'll sort it out right away.

choices: A delay and a booking mix-up

questions.q: What does the staff member do?

script.t: There's been a mix-up with my booking.
I'm sorry, I'll sort it out.

fluency432.prompt: Explain a travel problem and ask for help.

retell.prompt: Retell the traveller's problem at the desk.

task.prompt: Report a travel problem at a desk and ask them to fix it.

writing.model: My flight was delayed and there's been a mix-up with my booking. Could you sort it out, please?

sentenceCombining.answer: My flight was delayed, and there was a mix-up.
It's not working, so could you sort it out?

task.prompt: Write a short complaint about a travel problem (3 sentences).

options: I'd like to make a complaint.

quiz.q: Ask someone to fix a problem.

title: Then and now
canDo: I can compare past habits with the present using 'used to'.

chunks.use: Talk about a past habit or state.
chunks.example: I used to live abroad as a child.

chunks.text: I didn't use to like it.
chunks.example: I didn't use to like coffee.
Things have changed a lot since then.
Back then, life was slower.
These days, I'm busier than ever.

grammar.pattern: used to + verb / didn't use to + verb
examples: We didn't use to have phones.

reading.passage: I used to live abroad. Back then, life was slower and I didn't use to worry much. Things have changed: these days I'm busier, but I'm happier too.

questions.q: Where did the writer used to live?

script.t: Did you use to live here?
No, I used to live abroad.

gist.q: Where did B used to live?

minimalPair.note: 'used to' has an s-sound; 'use' (verb) has a z-sound.

fluency432.prompt: Compare your life in the past and now.

retell.prompt: Retell how the writer's life has changed.

task.prompt: Talk about how your life used to be and how it is now.

writing.model: I used to live abroad. Back then, life was slower. These days, I'm busier but happier.

sentenceCombining.answer: I used to live abroad, and life was slower.
Things have changed, and I'm busier now.

task.prompt: Write 3 sentences comparing your past and present using 'used to'.

title: Describing people and places
canDo: I can describe people and places using who, where, and that.

chunks.text: She's the one who helped me.
chunks.example: She's the one who helped me move.

chunks.text: It's the place where we met.
chunks.example: It's the cafe where we met.

chunks.text: That's the reason why I left.
chunks.example: That's the reason why I left.

chunks.use: Describe a person you rely on.
chunks.example: He's someone I trust completely.

chunks.text: The thing that matters is honesty.
chunks.example: The thing that matters is honesty.

grammar.pattern: the person who... / the place where... / the thing that...

reading.passage: She's the one who helped me when I arrived. The cafe is the place where we met. He's someone I trust completely. The thing that matters most is honesty, and that's the reason why we're still friends.

script.t: She's the one who helped me.

fluency432.prompt: Describe an important person and place in your life.

retell.prompt: Retell the description of the friend and the cafe.

task.prompt: Describe a person who helped you and a place that's special.

writing.model: She's the one who helped me. The cafe is the place where we met. He's someone I trust.

sentenceCombining.answer: She's a good friend who helped me.
This is the cafe where we met.

task.prompt: Describe two important people or places using who, where, that (3 sentences).

quiz.q: Describe the person who helped you.
options: She's the one who helped me.
It's the place where we met.
It's the place where we met.
